headline: Affordable Housing Programme Delivers Only 34% of Target
context: The Rumah Mampu Milik programme delivered only 47,600 units of its 140,000-unit target for 2020-2025, with delays blamed on land costs, contractor failures, and cross-agency coordination breakdowns.

cards.big: Only 34% delivery rate — 92,400 promised affordable homes were never built over 5 years
cards.sub: 1.2 million applications received for 47,600 completed units — 25 families competing for every home

cards.big: Average affordable housing construction cost rose 38% from RM180,000 to RM248,000 per unit since 2020
cards.sub: Land costs in Selangor and Penang make sub-RM300,000 pricing mathematically impossible without state land

cards.big: Waiting list for affordable housing in KL exceeds 380,000 families — average wait time is 7.2 years
cards.sub: Young couples delay marriage and childbirth citing inability to secure housing as primary reason

cards.big: 12 different agencies run affordable housing programmes with overlapping mandates and no single database
cards.sub: PR1MA, SPNB, PPR, and state agencies each maintain separate applicant lists creating duplication and confusion

cards.big: 12 agencies building homes means 12 agencies blaming each other when homes don't get built
Should all affordable housing programmes be merged under one federal agency with a single waiting list?
